summary: 2026-07-12 14:30 PDT — The code and test deletions match the approved retirement scope, but the updated live documentation incorrectly presents `closeRenderingContext` as an observable signal for Zoom-initiated teardown, contrary to the spec’s required distinction between recovery and observability.

findings.title: Replacement teardown signal cannot observe Zoom-initiated teardown
findings.claim: The changed guide says a `closeRenderingContext` log means the camera context went away because either the user clicked Hide or Zoom tore it down. That log is emitted only when application code calls `closeRenderingContext`; a Zoom hard-kill produces no such call or log. The same overclaim appears in the matrix’s changed teardown row at line 61. This contradicts the binding design decision that teardown is handled by recovery/extrapolation, not observed, and could cause live-test operators to treat silence during an actual Zoom teardown as evidence that no teardown occurred.
findings.suggestion: Describe `closeRenderingContext` strictly as confirmation of the app-initiated Hide path. State explicitly that Zoom-initiated teardown remains unobservable after retiring the pagehide breadcrumb and is instead mitigated by extrapolation and camera recovery.
